name: Dick Hyman
abstract: Richard Hyman (born March 8, 1927) is an American jazz pianist and composer. Over a 70-year career, he has worked as a pianist, organist, arranger, music director, electronic musician, and composer.

quotes.text: The closest I got to being a traditional on-the-road band leader was when I was directing the group which was then called “The New York Jazz Repertory Company” and we went to Russia, the Soviet Union that is, it wasn’t just Russia. And this was in 1975 and it was a remarkable experience. We did our Louis Armstrong show, which we had done previously in Carnegie Hall. In Moscow, Novorossiysk, which is way the heck over North of India, Alma Ata, which is in the south now, and Kazakhstan, all these places which are coming into the news now unfortunately, and one or two other spots. And we toured in buses or on maybe once or twice on a Russian plane. And it was an amazing introduction to the hazards and hardships of being a leader of a big band, doing that in Russia. © Fillius Jazz Archive -11-
quotes.caption: Mentions prior Carnegie Hall performance of Louis Armstrong show

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Well, Dick Hyman was one of your students, wasn't he? [INTERVIEWEE] Yes, Dick was one of my students.

about_quotes.speaker: Ken Peplowski
about_quotes.evidence: Because I talked to Dick Hyman about this, my friend, I talk to him a couple of times a week.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ken Peplowski interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 9, 2020 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Butch Miles
about_quotes.evidence: Dick Hyman is such a gentleman that when the album was released he sent all of us a note and he said — me and Milt and Bucky, he said, “Because you set it up and you guys were the ones who came up with the Basie ideas, I’m giving you co-writing rights on this one so that you’ll also get a little bit
about_quotes.doc_title: Butch Miles interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 9, 2020 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Flanigan
about_quotes.evidence: I’m leaving for home, I’ve got a concert with Dick Hyman, we’ve got a rehearsal with him Tuesday morning... So then we add Dick in there, Dick comes out and does all his little stride things... I do that with him
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Flanigan interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 15, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: Then's when I got together with Dick Hyman... Dick made the arrangement, and in the mean time I asked all the guys in the band... we played it down... Dick had the arrangement, and we played it down

about_quotes.speaker: Loren Schoenberg
about_quotes.evidence: Dick Hyman was going to put together a band... Then he heard my band and he wound up hiring my band and we became the last Benny Goodman Orchestra... Dick Hyman took the place of Dick Katz.
about_quotes.doc_title: Loren Schoenberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019
